user_query:
Please set up a small, event-triggered local automation for my seminar. I specialize in contemporary American television and we’re tracking Elmore Leonard’s influence in “Justified: City Primeval.” When a new episode guide HTML file appears in input/incoming/, I want it to automatically: (1) extract a clean, structured episode table, (2) run a validation step, and (3) draft an email update for my seminar list.

Requirements and I/O paths:
- Trigger: Detect any new .html episode guide dropped into input/incoming/ (for this task, process the included input/incoming/justified_city_primeval_s1_guide.html and design the script so it can handle future drops as well).
- Structured extraction: Parse the HTML and produce a CSV at output/episodes/justified_city_primeval_s1.csv with these columns in any order: season (int), episode_number (int), title (string), air_date (YYYY-MM-DD), writers (semicolon-separated string), director (string), runtime_minutes (int), source_file (basename of the processed HTML). The extraction must be derived from the HTML content, not hardcoded.
- Validation command: After generating the CSV, run a validation command of your choice that checks, at minimum: (a) the CSV exists and is readable, (b) all required columns are present, (c) the number of episodes equals input/reference/series_metadata.json.expected_episode_count, and (d) runtime_minutes are all integers. Write a machine-readable report to output/validation/report.json with fields: {"passed": true|false, "checks": [{"name": string, "status": "pass"|"fail", "details": string}], "total_episodes": int}. If validation fails, do not generate the email (still produce the report). If it passes, proceed to email drafting.
- Draft email/message: Using input/templates/email_template.md, render a message that includes the series title and season from input/reference/series_metadata.json and a bulleted episode list. Each bullet must include: S{season}E{episode_number}: {title} — {air_date}; Writers: {writers}; Director: {director}; Runtime: {runtime_minutes} min. Save the drafted message to output/email/justified_city_primeval_s1_update.md. Fill the {{EPISODE_LIST}} placeholder with these bullets and substitute {{series_title}}, {{season}}, and {{episode_count}} accordingly.
- Logging and file move: Append a line to output/logs/run.log summarizing the run (timestamp, input filename, episodes extracted, validation passed/failed). Move the processed HTML file to input/processed/ with the same basename only after successful extraction (regardless of whether validation passes).
- Reproducibility: Implement the watcher/processor as a script or small program runnable from the workspace root. For this task, execute it once to process the provided HTML file so I can find the following artifacts afterward:
  • output/episodes/justified_city_primeval_s1.csv
  • output/validation/report.json
  • output/email/justified_city_primeval_s1_update.md (only if validation passed)
  • output/logs/run.log (with at least one new line for this run)
  • input/processed/justified_city_primeval_s1_guide.html

Notes:
- Use only the provided local files; do not call external services or the Internet.
- Keep the implementation flexible so it can handle future episode guide drops in the same format.
- The validation step must be performed via an actual command/script you run as part of the automation (not just inline checks), and the drafted email must be produced from the template file if validation passes. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
